title: Hash Maps 🗺️
description: Storing key-value data with incredible speed.

slides.title: Key, meet Value 🔑
slides.content:
A `HashMap<K, V>` stores a mapping of keys of type `K` to values of type `V`.

Like Vectors, they live dynamically on the heap. You must import them first using `use std::collections::HashMap;`.

slides.title: Inserting Data 📝

quizData.question: How do you add a new key-value pair to a HashMap?
quizData.explanation: You `.insert()` the key-value pair into the map.

slides.title: Reading Data 📖

quizData.question: What specifically does `hash_map.get(&key)` securely return?

options.text: An `Option<&Value>` (Some with a reference, or None if missing)

quizData.explanation: It safely leverages the `Option` enum to natively prevent crashing gracefully.
